children.heading: Grants
children.label: a Grants
children.text: In addition to the authority of the Secretary under , the Secretary, acting through the Service, is authorized to provide grants to public or private schools of nursing for the purpose of establishing, developing, operating, and administering clinics to address the health care needs of Indians, and to provide primary health care services to Indians who reside on or within 50 miles of Indian country, as defined in .section 1616e(a)(1) of this titlesection 1151 of title 18

establish clinics, to be run and staffed by the faculty and students of a grantee school, to provide primary care services in areas in or within 50 miles of Indian country (as defined in );section 1151 of title 18
provide clinical training, program development, faculty enhancement, and student scholarships in a manner that would benefit such clinics; and
carry out any other activities determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Amount and conditions
children.label: c Amount and conditions
children.text: The Secretary may award grants under this section in such amounts and subject to such conditions as the Secretary deems appropriate.

children.heading: Design
children.label: d Design
children.text: The clinics established under this section shall be designed to provide nursing students with a structured clinical experience that is similar in nature to that provided by residency training programs for physicians.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Authorization to use amounts
children.label: f Authorization to use amounts
children.text: Out of amounts appropriated to carry out this subchapter for each of the fiscal years 1993 through 2000 not more than $5,000,000 may be used to carry out this section.
